description_long: This is a cylindrical stone tower with a crenellated parapet at the top, reminiscent of medieval fortifications. The exterior surface is textured with a pattern of rectangular stone blocks, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The parapet features evenly spaced battlements, providing defensive cover along the upper rim. There are no visible windows or doors, and the structure appears solid and imposing. The color is a uniform dark gray, suggesting weathered stone or concrete. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing strength and durability.
description: A tall, cylindrical stone tower with a crenellated parapet and block-textured exterior, finished in dark gray.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical body and crenellated parapet, with no visible openings or decorations.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the same block pattern and parapet, maintaining a consistent appearance with no additional features.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to the previous, emphasizing the uniformity of the stonework and parapet.
description_view_3: Rear view mirrors the front and sides, with the same stone texture and crenellated top, lacking any visible entrances or windows.
